# Garner llms.txt

Garner is an agent-first, flat-file PHP CMS for building websites and small web
apps. This file is for LLMs and agents working on a site built with Garner. It is
not contributor guidance for changing Garner's CMS engine.

## Primary Working Areas

- `routes/` is the site tree. Each route is a directory.
- `routes/+site.json` stores site-level metadata such as the site title.
- A directory with `+page.json`, `+page.yaml`, or `+page.yml` is a page.
- `app/templates/` contains shared Twig templates.
- A page may use a co-located `+template.twig` to override its template.
- A page may use a co-located `+controller.php` for page-specific data or a
  custom response.
- A page may use a co-located `+action.php` to handle its form POST.
- `config/app.php` contains project configuration.
- `public/` is the web root. Do not place source content here.
- `runtime/` contains derived indexes and caches. Treat it as disposable.

Avoid editing `src/`, `boot/`, `bin/`, `tests/`, or Composer metadata unless the
task is explicitly to change Garner itself rather than build the current site.

## Pages And Routes

The filesystem path under `routes/` defines the public route:

- `routes/+page.json` routes to `/`.
- `routes/about/+page.json` routes to `/about`.
- `routes/blog/post/+page.json` routes to `/blog/post`.

Canonical route paths do not use a trailing slash, except for `/`.

`+page.json` has no required fields. Common fields are:

- `id`: stable page identifier. Use this for references between pages.
- `title`: human-readable page title.
- `template`: shared Twig template name from `app/templates/`.
- `draft`: when `true`, the page 404s publicly and is hidden from listings.
- `sort`: integer ordering value. Lower values sort first.
- `created`: non-empty timestamp string when useful for ordering or display.

Keep route identity, page identity, ordering, and presentation separate. Do not
rename directories just to reorder navigation; use `sort` or project-specific
metadata.

## Content Files

Content lives beside the page entry file:

- `main.md` becomes `content.main`.
- `intro.md` becomes `content.intro`.
- `data.json` becomes `content.data`.
- `settings.yaml` or `settings.yml` becomes `content.settings`.

Markdown, text, JSON, and YAML files are parsed into the `content` namespace
unless they are reserved files or media sidecars.

Files beginning with `+` or `.` are reserved. Do not create arbitrary public
content files with those prefixes.

## Templates

Twig templates render pages. Shared templates live in `app/templates/`.

Typical template usage:

```twig
<h1>{{ page.title }}</h1>
{{ content.main|markdown }}
```

Useful values in templates:

- `page`: the current page.
- `site`: site data and traversal helpers.
- `content`: parsed content files for the current page.
- `page.url`: absolute URL.
- `page.path`: route path only.
- `site.children`: home plus direct children, excluding drafts.
- `site.index`: home plus all descendants, excluding drafts.
- `page.children`: direct child pages, excluding drafts.
- `site.findById(id)`: resolve a stable page id.

Use `|markdown` when rendering Markdown content. Prefer escaping/default Twig
behavior for ordinary values.

## Navigation And Listings

Garner listings exclude drafts and sort by `sort`, then route path. Filter
project-specific visibility with freeform metadata instead of inventing global
publication states.

Example:

```twig
{% for child in site.children.reject(child => child.get('nav') is same as false) %}
  <a href="{{ child.url }}">{{ child.title ?? child.id }}</a>
{% endfor %}
```

Use stable page ids for cross-page references. Avoid hard-coding URLs when a
relationship should survive a page move.

## Media And Files

Non-content files beside a page are page-owned assets:

```text
routes/about/
├── +page.json
├── main.md
├── team.jpg
└── team.jpg.json
```

Use page file accessors in Twig:

```twig
{% set photo = page.file('team.jpg') %}
{% if photo %}
  <img src="{{ photo.url }}" alt="{{ photo.get('alt') }}">
{% endif %}
```

Sidecar metadata is optional and lives in a sibling file such as
`team.jpg.json`, `team.jpg.yaml`, or `team.jpg.yml`.

Calling `file.url()` publishes the file to `public/media/<hash>/` and makes it
publicly downloadable. Do not call `url()` for private or access-controlled
files; stream those through a controller with an access check.

## Controllers And Endpoints

A co-located `+controller.php` may return:

- an array, merged into the Twig context;
- a `Garner\Render\RenderedResponse`, which bypasses Twig.

A `RenderedResponse` is immutable; chain `withHeader(name, value)` and
`withCookie(name, value)` for extra response headers and cookies.

Read the current request through `$app->request()` instead of PHP
superglobals: `method()`, `form()`, `json()`, `body()`, `cookie(name)`,
`header(name)`, `file(name)`, and `isHtmx()`.

Cross-site form POSTs are rejected with a 403 by default (origin check;
stateless CSRF protection). Same-origin forms, JSON APIs, and non-browser
clients are unaffected. Off-switch: `app.csrf.check_origin => false`.

## Form Actions

A co-located `+action.php` handles the page's POST (the write side; keep the
controller read-side). It returns a callable `(request, page, site, app)` and
must produce one of:

- `Garner\Render\ActionResult::failure(data, status = 422, fragment = null)` —
  re-render the page with `data` available in the template as `form`.
  `fragment` names a Twig block in the page template; an htmx POST is then
  answered with just that block (same context and status) so the form swaps
  in place. The block renders alone (`{% set %}` outside it does not run),
  so keep fragment blocks self-contained — compute derived values in the
  controller or inside the block. htmx ignores `4xx` responses by default —
  include the `htmx-config` meta tag that allows `422` to swap (see README,
  "Form actions") whenever a site uses fragment failures;
- `Garner\Render\ActionResult::redirect(location, status = 303)` — the
  Post/Redirect/Get success path. htmx-aware: an htmx POST (`HX-Request`)
  is answered with `204` + `HX-Redirect` instead of a `3xx`, so htmx
  navigates the whole page rather than swapping the redirect target into
  the form's `hx-target`;
- a `RenderedResponse` — escape hatch for JSON, HTMX fragments, or custom
  headers.

The `form` template variable is always defined (`null` on plain GET). `HEAD`
routes like `GET`. A POST without `+action.php` (or any other unhandled verb)
returns `405 Method Not Allowed` with an `Allow` header, unless a controller
answers it with a `RenderedResponse`. For a success message across a
redirect, use `$app->session()->flash(...)` (see "Sessions" below) rather
than baking it into the URL.

A directory with `+controller.php` and no page entry file is a route endpoint.
Use this for JSON APIs, feeds, sitemaps, or similar routes that should not appear
in the page tree.

## Sessions

`$app->session()` is per-visitor key/value state: `get(key, default)`,
`set(key, value)`, `has(key)`, `remove(key)`, `flash(key, value)` (readable
on the next request only, then expired whether or not it was read),
`consumeFlash(key, default)` (read once and clear), `hasFlash(key)` (peek
without consuming), `regenerate()` (rotate the session id — call after a
login feature authenticates someone), `destroy()`. The key `_flash` is
reserved (it carries flash metadata internally); `set('_flash', ...)` throws.

Activation is lazy: a visitor whose requests never call `set()`, `flash()`,
or `destroy()` gets no cookie and stays fully stateless/cacheable. Data lives
in `storage/sessions` by default (one file per session, serialized with PHP's
`serialize()` — not JSON, since session files aren't hand-edited — via
`app.session.*` config). Not a "logged-in user" concept — a future auth
feature builds on this rather than replacing it.

## Validation And Local Checks

For site-building tasks, prefer these commands when available:

```sh
php bin/garner validate
php bin/garner reindex
composer test
```

When changing PHP code, also run:

```sh
composer analyze
composer lint
```

Use `composer start` to run the local server at `http://localhost:8040`.

## Caches And Generated Files

The source of truth is the file tree, not generated caches.

- `runtime/index.sqlite` is a rebuildable route index.
- Twig cache files under `runtime/cache/` are rebuildable.
- Published media under `public/media/` is derived from page-owned files.

After content, template, or deployment changes, `php bin/garner reindex` may be
needed. For production deploys, refresh derived caches with:

```sh
php bin/garner cache:clear
php bin/garner reindex
```

## Site-Building Principles

- Prefer simple, inspectable files over hidden state.
- Preserve existing content and metadata unless the task asks for a change.
- Keep Markdown prose in Markdown files and structured data in JSON or YAML.
- Add templates and controllers only when content files and shared templates are
  not enough.
- Keep public copy respectful when comparing Garner with other CMSs or tools.
- Do not treat `runtime/` or generated `public/media/` files as authoring
  surfaces.
